person.name: Bertrand III de La Tour D'auvergne

nameForms.fullText: Bertrand III de La Tour D'auvergne

person.userCertifiedLifeSummary:
Armoiries de la famille de La Tour d'Auvergne :
D'azur semé de fleurs de lys d'or et à la tour d'argent maçonnée et ouverte de sable.

Bertrand IV de La Tour d'Auvergne assista à la Bataille de Poitiers où le Roi Jean II le Bon fut fait prisonnier en 1356. Il fut l'un des otages désignés pour prendre la place du Roi lorsque celui-ci rentrerait encore en France.
 Etant encore Damoiseau, il avait rendu hommage à l'Evêque de Clermont en 1330 pour ses fiefs de Florac, Madic, Claviers et pour ses maisons de Riom et de Fayet. Il prit part en 1364 à la guerre entreprise par les Chevaliers de l'Ordre Teutonique contre la Lituanie. 
Il vivait encore en août 1368. Il avait épousé le 13 octobre 1320 Isabelle de Levis fille de Jean de Levis Seigneur de Mirepoix et de Constance de Foix qui lui apporta une dot de 20.000 livres. Elle mourut le 3 avril 1361 et fut ensevelie aux Cordeliers de Clermont.

Sources :
Naissance : Personne: Comte A.de Remacle Dictionnaire Généalogique des Familles d'Auvergne T 2 p 410 Famille: Comte A. de Remacle, Dictionnaire Généalogique des Familles d'Auvergne T 2 p 410 - Famille: Detlev Schwennick: Europäische Stammtafeln X 94
relevé 2023 JPR Teyssier Chalancon

zh.lifeSummary: 當Bertrand III de La Tour D'auvergne於1303年出生在法國奧維-o-阿爾培斯普-德-o拉 圖-達奧維時，他的父親Bernard de la Tour d'Auvergne III29歲，母親Béatrix de Rodez31歲。他於1320年10月20日與Isabelle II de Levis-Mirepoix在法國奧維-o-阿爾培斯普-德-o拉 圖-達奧維結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於大約1368年8月在他的家鄉享年65歲，埋葬在法國奧維-o-阿爾培斯普-德-o可勒爾蒙吉福藍德。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Bertrand III de La Tour D'auvergne于1303年出生在法国奧維-o-阿爾培斯普-德-o拉 圖-達奧維时，他的父亲Bernard de la Tour d'Auvergne III29岁，母亲Béatrix de Rodez31岁。他于1320年10月20日与Isabelle II de Levis-Mirepoix在法国奧維-o-阿爾培斯普-德-o拉 圖-達奧維结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和2个女儿的父母。他去世于大约1368年8月在他的家乡享年65岁，埋葬在法国奧維-o-阿爾培斯普-德-o可勒爾蒙吉福藍德。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: La
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Hung, Cuong, Anh, Thanh, Ha, Hoang, Tuan, Chau, Hao, Hien, Hoa, Hue, Nam, Phong, Sinh, Tuong, Min, Tam, Tanh, Thai, Tham, Tinh, Yeong.  Chinese Hong, Hang, Sang, Tong, Chan, Chi, Hwa Ja, Hyung, Kam, Ki Bong, Kin, Man.
Vietnamese: from the Chinese surname 羅, see   Luo   1.
Vietnamese (Lã): from the Chinese surname 呂, see Lu 6 and 3 below.

variants.name: Luo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Jian, Yi, Ping, Yong, Zhen, Feng, Hong, Ming, Jin, Bin, Chen, Hai, Lan, Min, Huan, Hung, Shen, Tian.

Mandarin form of the surname 羅, meaning ‘net for catching birds’ or ‘to collect’ in Chinese: (i) from Luo (羅), the name of an ancient state (located in present-day Hubei province). After it was annexed by the state of Chu during the early Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ), people from the state migrated southwards and adopted Luo (羅) as their surname. (ii) from the Po Duo Luo (破多羅) and Chi Luo (叱羅) families from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China, who changed their original surnames to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) from the personal name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅), leader of an ancient Turkic tribe who are believed to have migrated to China during the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ). His descendants inherited his name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅) as their surname and shortened it to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iv) from the name of one of the seven families from the Bandun Man, an ethnic group residing in ancient southwestern China (located mainly in present-day Sichuan province).

nameMeanings.name: Tour
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Arabic/Muslim Ramez, Ashraf, Bakr, Mohamed, Mostafa, Nabil, Samir, Abdalla, Adib, Ali, Amir, Ebrahim.  Romanian Sorin, Dragos.
Romanian: nickname from nour, a variant of nor ‘cloud’.

variants.name: Nur
meanings: Muslim: from a personal name based on Arabic nūr ‘light, illumination’. Al-Nūr ‘the Light’ is an attribute of Allah, giving rise to the compound name ʿAbd al-Nūr, ʿAbdul-Nūr (see Abdelnour ). The word nūr is also found in combinations such as Nūr-Allāh or Nurullāh ‘Light of Allah’, Nūr ud-Dīn or Nuruddin ‘Light of Religion’. Compare Noor , Nor , and Nour .

nameMeanings.name: Bertrand
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Medieval French variant of   Bertram  , imported to Britain by the Normans. In modern times it has been made famous by the English philosopher  Bertrand Russell  (  1872–1970 ), but is only in occasional use.

nameMeanings.name: III
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Japanese Yuko, Aiko, Hideaki, Hiroki, Hiroyuki, Isamu, Kaoru, Katsumi, Keiichiro, Kenji, Masako, Masayuki.
Japanese: variously written, sometimes with characters used phonetically; the most probable meanings are 仁井 ‘benevolent well’, 新居 ‘new residence’, or 新井 ‘new well’. It is not common in Japan.

en.lifeSummary: When Bertrand III de La Tour D'auvergne was born in 1303, in La Tour-d'Auvergne, Puy-de-Dôme, Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes, France, his father, Bernard de la Tour d'Auvergne III, was 29 and his mother, Béatrix de Rodez, was 31. He married Isabelle II de Levis-Mirepoix on 20 October 1320, in La Tour-d'Auvergne, Puy-de-Dôme, Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes, France. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 2 daughters. He died about August 1368, in his hometown, at the age of 65, and was buried in Clermont-Ferrand, Puy-de-Dôme, Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes, France.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Bertrand III de La Tour D'auvergne est né en 1303, à La Tour-d'Auvergne, Puy-de-Dôme, Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes, France, son père, Bernard de la Tour d'Auvergne III, avait 29 ans et sa mère, Béatrix de Rodez, avait 31 ans. Il a épousé Isabelle II de Levis-Mirepoix le 20 octobre 1320, à La Tour-d'Auvergne, Puy-de-Dôme, Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes, France. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé environ août 1368, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 65 ans, et a été enterré à Clermont-Ferrand, Puy-de-Dôme, Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes, France.

de.lifeSummary: Als Bertrand III de La Tour D'auvergne 1303 in La Tour-d'Auvergne, Puy-de-Dôme, Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes, Frankreich geboren wurde, war sein Vater Bernard de la Tour d'Auvergne III 29 und seine Mutter Béatrix de Rodez 31. Er hat am 20. Oktober 1320 in La Tour-d'Auvergne, Puy-de-Dôme, Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes, Frankreich Isabelle II de Levis-Mirepoix geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Er starb um August 1368 im Alter von 65 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort und wurde in Clermont-Ferrand, Puy-de-Dôme, Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes, Frankreich begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Bertrand III de La Tour D'auvergne è nato il nel 1303 nel La Tour-d'Auvergne, Puy-de-Dôme, Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes, Francia, il suo padre, Bernard de la Tour d'Auvergne III, aveva 29 anni e il suo madre, Béatrix de Rodez, aveva 31 anni. Lui sposato Isabelle II de Levis-Mirepoix il 20 ottobre 1320, nel La Tour-d'Auvergne, Puy-de-Dôme, Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes, Francia. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 2 figlie. Morì circa agosto 1368, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 65 anni, e fu sepolto a Clermont-Ferrand, Puy-de-Dôme, Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes, Francia.

ja.lifeSummary: Bertrand III de La Tour D'auvergneが1303年にフランスアウバーグネ-ルhoネ-アルペスプウイ-デ-ドoメラ トウル-ド'アウバーグンで生まれた時，父Bernard de la Tour d'Auvergne IIIは29歳，母Béatrix de Rodezは31歳であった。1320年10月20日にフランスアウバーグネ-ルhoネ-アルペスプウイ-デ-ドoメラ トウル-ド'アウバーグンでIsabelle II de Levis-Mirepoixと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼は約1368年8月に故郷で65歳で死去し，フランスアウバーグネ-ルhoネ-アルペスプウイ-デ-ドoメクラーモント-フエランドにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Bertrand III de La Tour D'auvergne(이)가 1303년 프랑스 오버그니-ho니-알프스 푸이-데-드o 라 토우-드'오버그니에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Bernard de la Tour d'Auvergne III(은)는 29세였고, 그의 어머니 Béatrix de Rodez(은)는 31세였다. 그는 1320년 10월 20일 프랑스 오버그니-ho니-알프스 푸이-데-드o 라 토우-드'오버그니에서 Isabelle II de Levis-Mirepoix(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 대략 1368년 8월 그의 고향에서 65세의 나이로 사망했으며 프랑스 오버그니-ho니-알프스 푸이-데-드o 클레몽-페랜드에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Bertrand III de La Tour D'auvergne nasceu em 1303, em La Tour-d'Auvergne, Puy-de-Dôme, Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes, França, seu pai, Bernard de la Tour d'Auvergne III, tinha 29 anos e sua mãe, Béatrix de Rodez, tinha 31 anos. Ele casou-se com Isabelle II de Levis-Mirepoix em 20 de outubro de 1320, em La Tour-d'Auvergne, Puy-de-Dôme, Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes, França. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 2 filhas. Ele faleceu aproximadamente agosto de 1368, em sua cidade natal, com 65 anos, e foi sepultado em Clermont-Ferrand, Puy-de-Dôme, Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes, França.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Bertrand III de La Tour D'auvergne родился в 1303 г. в местности ла тоур-д'аувиргн, пуи-де-дoм, аувиргне-рoне-алпесь, Франция, его отцу по имени Bernard de la Tour d'Auvergne III было 29 лет, а матери, которую звали Béatrix de Rodez, 31. Он женился на женщине по имени Isabelle II de Levis-Mirepoix 20 октября 1320 г. в местности ла тоур-д'аувиргн, пуи-де-дoм, аувиргне-рoне-алпесь, Франция. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 2 дочери. Он умер около август 1368 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 65 лет и был похоронен в местности клирмонт-фирранд, пуи-де-дoм, аувиргне-рoне-алпесь, Франция.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Bertrand III de La Tour D'auvergne nació en 1303, en La Tour-d'Auvergne, Puy-de-Dôme, Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes, Francia, su papá, Bernard de la Tour d'Auvergne III, tenía 29 años y su mamá, Béatrix de Rodez, tenía 31 años. Se casó con Isabelle II de Levis-Mirepoix el 20 de octubre de 1320, en La Tour-d'Auvergne, Puy-de-Dôme, Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes, Francia. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió aproximadamente agosto de 1368, en su pueblo, a la edad de 65 años, y fue sepultado en Clermont-Ferrand, Puy-de-Dôme, Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes, Francia.
